official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the block_entity_data packet body
Define the block position, block entity type registry id, and trusted compound tag body shape
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound block_entity_data frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns block_entity_data immediately after block_destruction.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the block_entity_data id to ClientboundBlockEntityDataPacket.

The official block_entity_data body is block position via BlockPos.STREAM_CODEC, block entity type via ByteBufCodecs.registry(Registries.BLOCK_ENTITY_TYPE), and tag via ByteBufCodecs.TRUSTED_COMPOUND_TAG.

The fixture uses the official private ClientboundBlockEntityDataPacket(BlockPos, BlockEntityType<?>, CompoundTag) constructor with a built-in chest type and empty CompoundTag, so it does not require initialized Level, BlockEntity, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded block position, decoded block entity type, decoded tag, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove block entity semantics, NBT schema, world/chunk state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer block entity semantics, NBT schema, world/chunk state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
